intro: Amplitude and Avo both show up under the product analytics category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Amplitude publishes an entry price of $61/mo (Plus); Avo does not publish one. Both sit at moderate lock-in. Developer experience scores 5/5 against 4/5. Amplitude fits teams working on enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Avo is a closer match when the job is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). Worth noting: Amplitude is explicitly not for startup with <1K users; Avo is explicitly not for simple projects. The honest trade-off: Amplitude trades off on startup with <1K users — PostHog free tier is more generous; Avo trades off on pre-product stage. On the plus side, Amplitude highlights enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Avo points to understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). Amplitude's documentation also calls out warehouse-native mode (Snowflake/BigQuery). Avo similarly notes data-driven product decisions. For teams evaluating product analytics options today, the decision usually hinges on whether Amplitude's profile or Avo's profile maps more cleanly to the pricing model, lock-in tolerance, and scale your team can live with.
quick_take: Amplitude is for enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts; Avo is for understanding user behavior (funnels, retention); decide on entry price.
verdict_a: Choose Amplitude if your project is enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off, strong SDKs and docs (5/5) are a priority.
verdict_b: Choose Avo if your project is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention), medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.

faq.a: Amplitude uses pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, and Avo uses undisclosed pricing with free tier. Amplitude publishes an entry price of $61/mo (Plus), and Avo does not, so a side-by-side cost comparison isn't possible from public data.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amplitude to Avo?
faq.a: Our data puts Amplitude at medium lock-in (proprietary data model, warehouse-native reduces lock-in), and Avo at medium lock-in (analytics schema exportable). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.

Amplitude scores 5/5 on developer experience in our data, while Avo scores 4/5, so Amplitude has the edge on docs and SDK quality by that measure. Still, run a small integration spike on both before deciding — team familiarity with a given SDK style often matters more than a one-point score gap.

faq.q: Is Avo a good alternative to Amplitude?
faq.a: Avo is a reasonable alternative to Amplitude when your workload leans more toward understanding user behavior (funnels, retention) than enterprise. One caveat: Avo is explicitly not for simple projects, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
